item.title: Re: SCP-4581: The "Anti-Racist Audiocassette"
item.content:encoded: Very good SCP. Just one suggestion. For the partial testing logs, instead of using System of a Down's "Prison Song", use System of a Down's "Mr. Jack" as Mr. Jack more directly deals with police brutality as compared to prison song's more government based approach to the war on drugs and no direct relation to police. If you don't believe me, take a listen as Mr. Jack uses the line "Fuck you, Pig!" quite often, among other things. Trust me, this is the best possible choice in all of their songs, because i've listened to all of them since they're my favorite band. Otherwise, the SCP was a good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually it's good I was just in a bad mood

1+

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Oh my gosh, this is incredible. Thank you both, and thank you all, SCP Wiki! I never expected this kind of response for my first article, and I'm so incredibly pleased it struck a chord. (TRACK 6 FOREVER!)

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: oh dang, well done on the +100 Tsercele
br uh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this. Especially the description of how they became able to create anomalies. Very well written!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
M is quite insufferable.
Hope that is intentional.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang! I'm beyond flattered, thank you so much. Happy to contribute to the GoI, and I'm excited to contribute more to the wiki!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy heck! Thank you! That's high praise indeed! I'm glad you like Masterson; I've got some plans for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep falling in love with GAW and this is right up there with Kenowhere. This is a fantastic first article and I can't wait to read more from you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is definitely one of my favorite articles of Series V, and probably on the entire site. I'm probably a little biased because I love Dead Kennedys, RAtM, and anti-fascism, but this was incredibly well-written. The anomaly alone would've been pretty interesting in my opinion, but the chat logs and interrogation took this to a solid 10/10. A very strong +1

+ Mild Spoilers
- Mild Spoilers

Also Mx. Masterson is absolutely the nonbinary representation this site deserves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so good and with the tale attached is priceless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keneq is not unique: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/classification-committee-memo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like all the references and is a good concept.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Torn on this one, and deliberately avoiding reading other comments before I make mine.

I like Gamers Against Weed as a GOI. Both on a narrative level and on a more meta one for driving a lot of undesirable people off the site.

However, I'm of the opinion that they're really difficult to write well. The GAW continuity is one where the Foundation are kind of bad guys by default by virtue of being authoritarian in structure, there's nothing wrong with that per se but it needs to be compelling and I'm not entirely sure this gets there. Some of it feels like it's trying a little hard. Let me think

I feel like the *intent*, and correct me if I'm wrong, is that we're supposed to broadly agree with Mx. Masterson and find Gore sort of a sympathetic, maybe even pitiable figure, who is part of a system that she is beginning to maybe think she shouldn't be, and as I write that out I'm kinda half-talking myself into upvoting it, because ruminating on it, I do think you get that part across pretty well.

I do think the dialog could use another pass, but I struggle to articulate my criticism beyond "it sells Masterson a little too hard as a character". We can tell we're supposed to sympathize with them and I think maybe you overcommit a little bit, I'm not sure they need to lay out their entire political ideology, which they kind of do. Most people who've been on the site for a bit know GAW's general deal.

I would say, on another note, I think the description of the anomaly *itself* could be clearer. Kuz right now it is a little confusing.

Also is this the first skip to reference its secondary containment class (the mention of it being Keneq) in the body of the article? I think it's the first I've seen.

In any case I've talked myself into a +1.

Certainly it's very good for a first skip. I hope I can write one this good when/if ever I take the plunge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the upvote and comment! Plus, the completely valid take! There's a muddy moral dimension here that I find interesting.

One thing that I tried to do with the piece (with limited success) is establish that Masterson (despite their preachiness) is absolutely not a neutral actor, any more than Gore is a neutral actor. Gore is absolutely correct in saying that Masterson has built a bomb; it has a safety, but it's an inadequate and incomplete check on SCP-4581's power. (I mentally compared it to kektagon's ill-fated Nerfing Gun. It doesn't have more specific or explicit limitations because oh god, why would you even use it like that?!)

Like you say, this cassette could very easily be a Keter-class anomaly. Slip this thing into a televised press conference and watch international news networks tumble like dominoes as they share each other's footage. Masterson never, ever anticipated this, because they built the cassette for a very specific purpose (to be elaborated upon in a Tale) and thought they'd hold onto it forever. When PIC talked about his dilemma, Masterson thought they had an easy fix; loan it out, zap the guy, get it back. Their observer bias meant they didn't realize the potential repercussions. This is an extremely common problem for GAW, but also for Masterson in particular, who has extremely strong convictions yet can't see past the end of their nose sometimes.

(Point of clarification: the trigger conditions aren't based on musical taste. It's entirely based on whether you (1) record SCP-4581-A, and (2) upload said recording anywhere. The musical selections are anti-racist/anti-fascist/anti-authority because they're an extension of Masterson's intent while "pakooshing" it. They're telling the truth when they say it's an reflection of the operator… technically… but it's not a perfect mirror. It's more like 60% operator, 40% Masterson's attitude.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting anomaly, and I always enjoy the GAW irc logs, especially bones, but i also always struggle to sympathise with GAW protagonists who portray themselves as morally superior when creating anomalies with no safeties.
This thing is a Keter that could theoretically obliterate all media someone possessed for the 'crime' of not liking punk, if I understand the trigger conditions correctly, yet that is consistent with GAW's rather juvenile understanding of how to approach politics.
However, just because Masterson annoys _me_, doesn't make this a poor SCP, so upvote for clever concept with slightly shaky execution.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Praise Sigmar, this comment is in violation of our site's criticism policy.

Terrible, overused political rhetoric that I'm getting soo sick and tired of seeing.

Politics are an inherent quality of fiction and always have been. Just because you disagree with the views expressed in a specific article, that doesn't mean you can dismiss it on those grounds. If you actually had a salient criticism of the delivery of said views, we wouldn't be doing this right now.

GaW is the worst God damn thing to happen to this website since gender-confused self insert satalite.

This is a bad faith argument and I will not be entertaining it.

This is a CLOSED staff post. Do not reply unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Terrible, overused political rhetoric that I'm getting soo sick and tired of seeing. Seriously. GaW is the worst God damn thing to happen to this website since gender-confused self insert satalite.
However, your writing skills and in particular the way you write character dialogue is quite good, so I guess I can't down vote.
Novote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great first SCP! The early presentation was nice (though I personally didn't think the blue text was necessary), going into its effects within the Foundation network, ending with an enjoyable interview. The interplay between the two was engaging and felt natural.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh gosh, thank you! Happy to drop a reference - your Canada-focused SCPs really leapt out at me and seemed like a natural fit for what I had in mind. (1st Midnight and Karina Kimura are particular favorites!)

As an aside: I didn't want to sign that email "Site-201 Director", because I wasn't sure whether you had established a director in your writing. So I went with "Interim Director" instead. Similarly, the name started off as a reference (to Marc Laidlaw of Half-Life fame), then I made it gender-neutral, then I realized it had become a reference again (to M.L.A. formatting). So there you go, that's a little glimpse of my process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from flipping out when I saw my MTF and Site referenced in here, I absolutely adore this. Track 6 forever.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God this kicks so much ass. Love it. +1.

Edit: i think the spectrogram is what did it for me. Just something to consider.
Gotta to agree with the others. The spectrogram is my favorite part of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good SCP, Good Taste in music, Good Propaganda

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Extremely happy to oblige! GAW seems to exist at the intersection of a particularly great "trans/leftist/shitposting" Venn diagram and I am very pleased to contribute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the early crits I received pointed out that there was no obvious link between the tape and GAW, making the safehouse raid seem abrupt. That stumped me for a while, because GAW normally doesn't sign their stuff… unless… it weren't visible in the first place! Putting that together was fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That spectrogram is nutty and sealed the upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my favorite GOI with one of my favorite bands doing one of my favorite things featuring a cool adult trans character?? all in a good character piece with a good backing anomaly?????

easiest upvote of my life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My understanding of the situation under the cut:

+ show block
– hide block

JJ invited bones (Eli, or 2721-LORD) to be moderator when GAW was getting off the ground. Though some/most of the others know, they're not entirely sure whether bones is actually an AI, or whether they're just being trolled. (bones insists that they only tell the truth.)

Since this is my first piece, I didn't want to make assumptions about who knows what and when, so 1) the dates are deliberately fuzzy, and 2) steakshift is simply ignorant, for whatever reason. Perhaps they'll hear about it someday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! Looking forward to contributing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh, thank you! I'm so glad you enjoyed it. I've been reading SCP for years, but once I discovered GAW, I knew I wanted to contribute. It really speaks to me.

(Plus, thanks for reminding me of that detail! Characterization is important. I'll fix that promptly.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…

+ show block
– hide block

Does this mean that other GAW moderators know of bones' alien nature, and is he 2721-LORD or -LYRE?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fantastic SCP and a very good start. looking forward to more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a lot of emotions about this. I don’t know exactly what to say now. Obviously I love it. I love love love the spectrogram.

Edit: a weird, anal correction. Bluntfiend speaks with capitals and perfect syntax. Like an old man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! I'm really pleased with how it turned out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely could not resist

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't link it in the chatlogs (since in my mind, steakshift doesn't know) but there's a reason bones is the best, most impartial moderator for GAW. Perspective indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so glad to see this up now, and it's really great that this is rising so fast!

As they say in a foreign land, "See you in hot!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love how you added a Rick roll joke in there: "Results: "Paparazzi", Lady Gaga (60 dB); "Don't Look Any Further", Dennis Edwards (60 dB); "Never Gonna Give You Up", Rick Astley (65 dB)."
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
bones: From my perspective, thirty-two terrestrial years does not seem like a long time.

hol up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thanks for reading and commenting! I'm so glad you enjoyed! In earlier drafts, there was more overt passive-aggressive conflict between Researcher Gore and RAISA, on account of her accidentally destroying a fair amount of work. This was pared back in the editing process, leaving just enough to dog Penelope's record going forward. Alas poor Tipper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a love-hate relationship w/ GAW these past few weeks. I really enjoy it, but it also reminds me of a lot of things I rather not remember, so I will have to no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good! The dialogue feels natural, the writing, both in the conprocs and in the description is excellent, and the narrative is very interesting. If I had to point out an issue, I would say that I didn't feel that the blue "updated procedures" are necessary, but I might just have missed something, and anyway it doesn't really affect the very good content. Overall, an easy +1.

item.title: SCP-4581: The "Anti-Racist Audiocassette"
item.content:encoded:
It's my first SCP! I've been reading the wiki for a long time and I'm thrilled to finally contribute. Many thanks go out to cybersqyd and DrAkimoto for providing detailed feedback and greenlighting my concept, and to kinchtheknifeblade - for taking an early look, and for developing such a fun GoI!

A peek behind the curtain: A few months ago, I heard that someone had used copyrighted music to stymie a would-be propagandist, and I thought that was a brilliant nonviolent solution. Eventually, I realized that was the same kind of thing Gamers Against Weed would appreciate, and I started thinking about delivery media. I've always loved the audacious visual design of In God We Trust, Inc., and this seemed like an obvious match… particularly when I realized the younger members of GAW would inevitably clown on its creator for being behind the times. The character of "steakshift" emerged from the ensuing back-and-forth.

There are a few deliberate themes in this piece: the omnipresence of the observer effect, culture clash, the fuzzy definition of "non-violence", and the inevitable ripple effect of all human action. Like many anomalies, this one does exactly what the designer intended… just a bit too well.

There's a few niche references scattered through this piece (the Tipper Gore joke being most obvious). I wonder if people will spot them?

Attributions: The cassette image is composited from three different CC assets:

  • Base: Ubcule's retouched compact cassette (CC 0);
  • Detail: Thegreenj's compact cassette (CC 2.5/ CC 3);
  • Logo: Tankist-777's anti-racist flag (CC 0).

I used alexadam's img-encode program to write the "Stephen King, hit by car" joke onto an audio spectrogram.

Also, credit where it's due: the "MxMasters" alias is inspired by one of @mcclure111's jokes on Twitter.

Update: SCP-4581 now has an accompanying Tale, set in the immediate aftermath of steakshift's interrogation! I hope you enjoy stoners riffing on movie footage, and also deep emotional turmoil: "Smile! You Are Being Watched!"
